abstract: Selecting and validating robust classification and regression models remains a fundamental challenge in computational research. While cross-validation is the standard approach, many current state-of-the-art methodologies produce models characterized by high variance, which limits their utility in sensitive practical applications such as Quantitative Structure-Activity Relationship (QSAR) modeling. In this paper, we describe and evaluate a set of best practices designed to enhance model reliability and bolster confidence in the predictive performance of selected frameworks. A key operational component of the proposed workflow is the integration of rigorous error estimation and stability metrics, which ensure that the resulting models are both statistically sound and generalizable across diverse datasets.
